summary: Older Adult Center participation was still recovering from COVID-19 pandemic closures in this period. NYC Aging's own reporting describes a campaign to "encourage and reassure older New Yorkers to safely return to Older Adult Centers" after the pandemic disrupted services and visitation, and credits that effort for a jump in attendance as centers worked back toward pre-pandemic participation levels, which explains most of the rise from 92,600 participants in FY22 to roughly 150,000 in FY24-25.

sources.label: NYC Department for the Aging, Fiscal 2023 Mayor's Management Report
